content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Pokémon Army (easy version)". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1420/C1
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of pokemons. The second line contains n space-separated integers, representing the levels of the pokemons.
content.outputFormat: The maximum number of pokemons that can be selected.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the levels of the pokemons in ascending order and then select the pokemons with the smallest levels until the total level exceeds the target level.

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
arr.sort()
sum = 0
count = 0
for i in range(n):
    sum += arr[i]
    if sum <= 100:
        count += 1
print(count)
